program_en: B.Sc. in Biotechnology, Entrepreneurship and Healthcare Management
program_chi: 生物科技、創業與醫療管理理學士
learning_outcomes:
1 1. Major Programme:
The programme aims to equip
students with entrepreneurial mindset for producing ground-breaking ideas,
integrated knowledge in life/health sciences, data analytics and business
management to provide innovative/interdisciplinary solutions, and also
ability to communicate with collaborators of diverse background and
manage/advance/provide solution for healthcare operations and translate
medical/scientific advances into practice.
Upon completion of the programme, students will have:
Knowledge outcomes
(1)  |  acquired understanding on state-of-the-art biotechnology
and application perspective;
(2)  |  understood the fundamental concepts in business
management with advanced knowledge in data analytics and financial
management;
(3)  |  acquired the contemporary concepts of public health and
the interactions between determinants of health at individual, local and
global levels;
(4)  |  explained from global perspective the burden of disease,
the links between health and development, and approaches to international
cooperation to monitor, promote and protect health; and
(5)  |  described important issues involved in public health
advocacy, including ethical reasoning, civic engagement, and social justice.
Skills outcomes
(1)  |  analyzed critical information related to biotechnology
and make business plan;
(2)  |  used data analytics tools to make data-driven decisions
and formulate strategies;
(3)  |  demonstrated leadership skills and can communicate
effectively with stakeholders in both public and commercial sectors to
facilitate collaborations and manage innovation processes;
(4)  |  assessed the health status, social concern, needs and
equity of different populations contributing to the use of health services
and health promotion practices; and
(5)  |  utilized evidence-based approach to inquire, analyze and
criticize contemporary public health issues, and design framework for
primary/secondary/tertiary care and prevention.
Values and attitude outcomes
(1)  |  had a positive attitude in applying biotechnology to
benefit the human society and contribute to sustainable development;
(2)  |  applied market orientation in both public and private
sectors;
(3)  |  demonstrated appreciation of the importance of primary
care in population health;
(4)  |  acknowledged the cross-cultural impacts on actual and
perceived health needs; and
(5)  |  upheld core value of social equity and justice.
study_scheme:
Biotechnology, Entrepreneurship and Healthcare Management Biotechnology, Entrepreneurship and Healthcare
Management Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major
Programme Requirement
Students are
required to complete a minimum of 65 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package: LSCI1002, MEDF1012, MGNT1020  |  9
2.  |  Required Courses: ACCT2111, BEHM4000,
DOTE1030, 2011#, 2030#,
FINA2010#, GRON4411#, MBTE2000#, 3000#, PHPC1001, 1017, 2016#, SBMS2104#  |  33
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  17
Students must
choose at least one concentration area by taking at least 17 units of
courses from the following:
(a)  |  Biotechnology Entrepreneurship and Innovation Required Courses: (8-9 units) ACCT2151# or
3151# or LAWS2331#, FINA3080#, MGNT4090# Elective Courses: Choose 9 units from the following: MBTE3510#,
3511#, 3518#, 3521#, 3528#, 3531#, 3550#, 3558#, 3560#, 3568#
(b)  |  Healthcare and Biomedicine Analytics
Required Courses: (11 units)
DOTE2040#,
4260#, PHPC3024#, SBMS3216#
Elective Courses:
Choose 6 units from the following:
DOTE4020#,
4110#, 4220#, GRON3412#, MGNT4010#, PHPC2017#, 3034#, 4032#, SBMS3101#,
3202#, 3205#, 3212#, 3215#
4.  |  Capstone Courses: BEHM4001, 4002  |  6
Total:  |  65
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  BEHM courses at 2000
and above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in the
calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are not
allowed to declare Minor in (i) Business; (ii) Public Health.
